# Experiment 3: Model Comparison for SCOUT Delegates

## Objective
Compare 4 models on the SCOUT research task (clouatre-labs/aptu#737) using 5 runs each (20 total).

## Models Under Test
- Baseline: claude-haiku-4-5 (GCP Vertex AI)
- Candidate A: qwen3-coder (OpenRouter)
- Candidate B: gemini-3-flash (GCP Vertex AI)
- Candidate C: devstral-2512 (OpenRouter)

## Blinding
- label-map.json is written before any runs and not read until scoring
- Output files use run numbers only (scout-run-NN.json)
- Orchestrator does not include model names in delegate instructions

## Concurrency
- Maximum 1 delegate at a time (avoids 5-delegate cap)
- Sequential execution only

## Retry Policy
- Maximum 5 attempts per run
- Retry only on invalid JSON output

## Scoring
- Scoring happens in a separate session after all 20 runs complete
- Orchestrator does not score

## Output
- Sessions: experiments/exp3-model-comparison/sessions/
- Latency log: experiments/exp3-model-comparison/latency-log.jsonl
- Label map: experiments/exp3-model-comparison/label-map.json (sealed before runs)
</tool_response>

<tool_response>
# aptu

A security-focused Rust CLI tool for analyzing package dependencies and detecting vulnerabilities.

## Overview

aptu (Automated Package Trust Utility) scans project dependencies for known vulnerabilities, license compliance issues, and supply chain risks. It integrates with multiple vulnerability databases and provides actionable remediation guidance.

## Features

- Dependency graph analysis
- CVE/vulnerability scanning via OSV, NVD, and GitHub Advisory databases
- License compliance checking
- Supply chain risk scoring
- Pattern-based security scanning
- Configurable severity thresholds

## Quick Start

```bash
cargo install aptu
aptu scan --path ./my-project
```

## Documentation

See [docs/](docs/) for full documentation.

---
[Cargo.toml]
[package]
name = "aptu"
version = "0.8.2"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.75"
authors = ["clouatre-labs"]
description = "Automated Package Trust Utility - security scanning for Rust projects"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
repository = "https://github.com/clouatre-labs/aptu"

[dependencies]
tokio = { version = "1.35", features = ["full"] }
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1.0"
reqwest = { version = "0.11", features = ["json", "rustls-tls"] }
clap = { version = "4.4", features = ["derive"] }
anyhow = "1.0"
thiserror = "1.0"
tracing = "0.1"
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter"] }
regex = "1.10"
toml = "0.8"
semver = "1.0"
rayon = "1.8"
indicatif = "0.17"
colored = "2.1"

[dev-dependencies]
tempfile = "3.8"
mockito = "1.2"
criterion = { version = "0.5", features = ["html_reports"] }

[features]
default = []
ast-scanning = ["tree-sitter", "tree-sitter-rust"]

[dependencies.tree-sitter]
version = "0.20"
optional = true

[dependencies.tree-sitter-rust]
version = "0.20"
optional = true
</tool_response>

# Contributing to aptu

## Commit Style
Conventional commits required: `type(scope): description`
Types: feat, fix, docs, test, refactor, chore, perf
All commits must be signed (GPG or SSH): `git commit -S`
DCO sign-off required: `git commit --signoff`

## Testing
- Unit tests: in `#[cfg(test)]` modules within source files
- Integration tests: `tests/` directory
- Run: `cargo test`
- Coverage: `cargo tarpaulin`

## Code Style
- `cargo fmt` before committing
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
- No `unwrap()` in production code -- use `?` or explicit error handling

## Error Handling
- Use `anyhow::Result` for application-level errors
- Use `thiserror` for library-level error types
- Propagate with `?`, add context with `.context()`
